New teachers are afraid of "running out" — so they pack 25 poses into 60 minutes. The result is a hectic experience where students never get to land in any pose.

The fix: Fewer poses, longer time. 10-15 poses are plenty for a 60-minute class. Give students time to find the pose, feel it, and breathe in it. Silence is not emptiness — it's space.

"New-teacher me wanted to fill every second with instruction. Experienced me knows that the best moments in a class are when nothing is said."

Mistake 3: You use too many words

"And now bend your right knee to 90 degrees, keep your left leg straight, press the outside of your left foot down, lift your arms up overhead, hands together, and look up at your thumbs..." Who can remember all that?

The fix: Cue in layers:

  1. First: the big movement ("Step one foot forward")
  2. Then: refinement ("Knee over ankle")
  3. Finally: the intention ("Feel the power in your legs")

Not all at once. Give students time between each layer.

Mistake 4: You copy your favourite teacher

It's natural — your favourite teacher's style inspired you to teach. But you're not them. And your students can tell the difference between authentic teaching and a copy.

The fix: Be inspired, but find your own voice. What are you passionate about? What's your strength? Maybe it's anatomical precision, maybe it's poetic cueing, maybe it's humour. Whatever it is — ownership beats imitation, every time.

Mistake 5: You don't set boundaries

New teachers want to please. So they teach for free, say yes to every time slot, accept low pay, and adapt to studio demands without negotiating.

The fix: You are a professional. Have a minimum rate. Have clear boundaries for when you're available. Respect your own time — if you don't, no one else will either.

The best mistake of all

Is to start. Perfectionism is the greatest enemy of new yoga teachers. You will make mistakes — and that's the point. Every mistake is feedback. Every class is a learning. The most important thing is that you teach, evaluate, and adjust.

Our yoga teacher training includes extensive practice teaching with individual feedback, so you can make your mistakes in a safe space. Or read our guide to cueing and voice use for more teaching tips.

entries.title_da: Cueing: Kunsten at Guide med Stemmen
entries.title_en: Cueing: The Art of Guiding with Your Voice
entries.excerpt_da: Din stemme er dit vigtigste undervisningsværktøj. Lær de fire typer cueing, stemmens kraft og de mest almindelige fejl i verbal instruktion.
entries.excerpt_en: Your voice is your most important teaching tool. Learn the four types of cueing, the power of voice, and the most common mistakes in verbal instruction.
entries.content_da:
Du kan have den perfekte sekvens, den smukkeste playliste og det mest indbydende rum — men uden god cueing falder det hele fra hinanden. Din stemme er det primære værktøj, du har som yogalærer. Den guider, motiverer, berolige og forbinder. At mestre cueing er forskellen mellem en god og en fantastisk lærer.

De fire typer cueing

1. Direktional cueing

Den mest grundlæggende form: at fortælle eleverne, hvad de skal gøre med kroppen.

  • "Trin dit højre ben frem"
  • "Løft armene over hovedet"
  • "Drej maven mod venstre"

Tips: Vær specifik. "Løft armen" er uklart — hvilken arm? Hvor højt? Brug retningssprog: op, ned, frem, tilbage, ind, ud.

2. Alignment cueing

Refinement af stillingen: at fortælle eleverne, hvordan de gør det korrekt.

  • "Knæ direkte over anklen"
  • "Skuldre væk fra ørerne"
  • "Bækken i neutral — hverken tilt fremad eller tilbage"

Tips: Brug landmærker på kroppen. "Bækken over hæle" er mere brugbart end "stå lige."

3. Billedsprog (imagery cueing)

Den mest kraftfulde form: at bruge billeder og metaforer til at fremkalde den rigtige fornemmelse.

  • "Rødder fra dine fødder ned i jorden"
  • "Løft brystet som en sol, der stiger"
  • "Slap af i ansigtet — som om du ser en solnedgang"

Tips: Billedsprog aktiverer andre dele af hjernen end direktional cueing. Det skaber en oplevelse, ikke bare en instruktion.

4. Intentional cueing

Det dybeste lag: at forbinde stillingen med en følelse, et formål eller en bevidsthed.

  • "Mærk styrken i dine ben — den styrke, du bærer med dig uden for studiet"
  • "I denne twist: hvad slipper du? Hvad holder du fast i?"
  • "Find stedet, hvor udfordring møder ro. Bliv der."
"God cueing fortæller kroppen, hvad den skal gøre. Fantastisk cueing fortæller mennesket, hvem det kan være."

Stemmens kraft

Det er ikke kun hvad du siger, men hvordan du siger det:

  • Tempo: Sænk tempoet i restorative dele. Øg det i dynamiske flows. Din stemme styrer klassens energi.
  • Volumen: Hvisk i Savasana. Projicér i Warrior. Variation holder opmærksomheden.
  • Pauser: Den mest undervurderede teknik. En pause efter en instruktion giver eleverne tid til at processere og reagere.
  • Tone: Varm og indbydende? Eller kommanderende og energisk? Begge har deres plads — men de skaber vidt forskellige oplevelser.

De 3 mest almindelige cueing-fejl

  1. Over-cueing: At tale hele tiden. Eleverne har brug for stilhed for at mærke kroppen.
  2. Vage instruktioner: "Find din egen variation" — hvad betyder det for en nybegynder? Giv konkrete alternativer.
  3. Manglende retningsfornemmelse: At sige "venstre" når du mener "højre" — det sker for alle. Mirror cueing (spejlvendt instruktion) kræver øvelse.

Øvelse gør mester

Cueing er en færdighed, der udvikles over tid. Optag dig selv. Lyt tilbage. Hvad virker? Hvad kan forbedres? Og vigtigst: undervise, undervise, undervise.

Vores yogalæreruddannelse dedikerer betydelig tid til cueing-træning med live feedback. Eller læs videre i vores journal for flere undervisningsfærdigheder.
entries.content_en:
You can have the perfect sequence, the most beautiful playlist, and the most inviting room — but without good cueing, it all falls apart. Your voice is the primary tool you have as a yoga teacher. It guides, motivates, calms, and connects. Mastering cueing is the difference between a good and a great teacher.

The four types of cueing

1. Directional cueing

The most basic form: telling students what to do with the body.

  • "Step your right foot forward"
  • "Lift your arms overhead"
  • "Rotate your belly to the left"

Tips: Be specific. "Lift your arm" is unclear — which arm? How high? Use directional language: up, down, forward, back, in, out.

2. Alignment cueing

Refinement of the pose: telling students how to do it correctly.

  • "Knee directly over ankle"
  • "Shoulders away from ears"
  • "Pelvis in neutral — neither tilting forward nor back"

Tips: Use body landmarks. "Pelvis over heels" is more useful than "stand straight."

3. Imagery cueing

The most powerful form: using images and metaphors to evoke the right sensation.

  • "Roots from your feet down into the earth"
  • "Lift your chest like a rising sun"
  • "Soften the face — as if watching a sunset"

Tips: Imagery activates different parts of the brain than directional cueing. It creates an experience, not just an instruction.

4. Intentional cueing

The deepest layer: connecting the pose with a feeling, a purpose, or an awareness.

  • "Feel the strength in your legs — the strength you carry with you beyond the studio"
  • "In this twist: what are you releasing? What are you holding onto?"
  • "Find the place where challenge meets calm. Stay there."
"Good cueing tells the body what to do. Great cueing tells the person who they can be."

The power of voice

It's not only what you say, but how you say it:

  • Tempo: Slow down in restorative sections. Speed up in dynamic flows. Your voice controls the class's energy.
  • Volume: Whisper in Savasana. Project in Warrior. Variation maintains attention.
  • Pauses: The most underrated technique. A pause after an instruction gives students time to process and respond.
  • Tone: Warm and inviting? Or commanding and energetic? Both have their place — but they create vastly different experiences.

The 3 most common cueing mistakes

  1. Over-cueing: Talking all the time. Students need silence to feel the body.
  2. Vague instructions: "Find your own variation" — what does that mean for a beginner? Give concrete alternatives.
  3. Directional confusion: Saying "left" when you mean "right" — it happens to everyone. Mirror cueing requires practice.

Practice makes perfect

Cueing is a skill that develops over time. Record yourself. Listen back. What works? What can improve? And most importantly: teach, teach, teach.

Our yoga teacher training dedicates significant time to cueing practice with live feedback. Or continue reading in our journal for more teaching skills.

entries.author: Yoga Bible
entries.title_en: Yoga as Side Hustle or Full-Time?
entries.excerpt_da: Kan du leve af yoga? Hvornår giver det mening at gå fra bijob til fuldtid? Her er den ærlige guide med tal, planlægning og realistiske forventninger.
entries.excerpt_en: Can you make a living from yoga? When does it make sense to go from side hustle to full-time? Here's the honest guide with numbers, planning, and realistic expectations.
entries.content_da:
"Kan man leve af at undervise i yoga?" Svaret er ja — men ikke fra dag ét, og ikke på den måde, de fleste forestiller sig. Denne guide giver dig de reelle tal, den ærlige tidshorisont og den praktiske plan for at gøre yoga til en bæredygtig karriere.

Yoga som bijob: De første 6-12 måneder

De fleste nye yogalærere starter her — og det er klogt. Her er, hvordan bijob-fasen typisk ser ud:

  • 2-4 klasser per uge ved et eller to studier
  • Honorar: 300-500 kr per klasse i København (mindre uden for byen)
  • Månedlig indkomst: 3.000-8.000 kr før skat
  • Tidsforbrug: 6-12 timer/uge inkl. forberedelse og transport

Bijob-fasen er vigtig, fordi den giver dig:

  1. Undervisningserfaring uden økonomisk pres
  2. Netværk — du møder studier, elever og andre lærere
  3. Feedback — du finder ud af, hvad der virker og hvad der ikke gør
  4. Tid til at udvikle din undervisningsstil

Overgangsperioden: Hvornår er du klar til at skalere?

Signalerne, der fortæller dig, at det er tid til at gøre mere:

  • Dine klasser er konsekvent fulde — folk anbefaler dig videre
  • Du får henvendelser fra studier, du ikke selv har kontaktet
  • Du har privatelever, der vil have mere tid med dig
  • Du føler dig begrænset af dit nuværende skema — du vil undervise mere
  • Din yoga-indkomst dækker mindst 30-40% af dine faste udgifter

Vejen til fuldtid: En realistisk plan

Diversificér dine indtægtsstrømme

Fuldtids yogalærere lever sjældent af kun studio-klasser. De kombinerer:

  • Studieundervisning: Fast base-indkomst
  • Privattimer: 500-1.000 kr/session — din højeste timesats
  • Corporate yoga: 800-2.000 kr/session — stabilt og godt betalt
  • Workshops: 2.000-5.000 kr for en 3-timers workshop
  • Retreats: 5.000-15.000 kr for en weekend
  • Online: Skalerbar indkomst fra video-indhold

Tal fra virkeligheden

En fuldtids yogalærer i København med 2+ års erfaring:

  • Typisk brutto: 20.000-35.000 kr/måned
  • Erfarne med corporate + retreats: 35.000-55.000 kr/måned
  • Top-tier: 55.000+ kr/måned (eget studie, online platform, retreats)
"Jeg gik fuldtid efter 18 måneder. Det krævede en opsparingsbuffer på 3 måneders udgifter og en detaljeret plan. I dag er min indkomst højere end mit gamle kontorist-job — og min livskvalitet er usammenlignelig."

De skjulte udgifter

Husk at medregne:

  • Forsikring: Erhvervsansvarsforsikring (1.500-3.000 kr/år)
  • Efteruddannelse: Workshops, certificeringer, konferencer
  • Udstyr: Matte, props, beklædning, musik-abonnement
  • Transport: Mellem studier og corporate-kunder
  • Markedsføring: Website, sociale medier, visitkort

Den ærlige konklusion

Kan du leve af yoga? Ja. Er det nemt? Nej. Er det det værd? Det afhænger af, hvad du søger. Hvis du vil have tryghed og forudsigelighed, er yoga som bijob en fantastisk livskvalitets-booster. Hvis du brænder for det og er villig til at investere 2-3 år i at bygge op, kan fuldtid være det bedste karrierevalg, du nogensinde træffer.

Vores yogalæreruddannelse inkluderer et "Business of Yoga"-modul, der giver dig de redskaber, de fleste uddannelser mangler. Eller kontakt os for at høre om vores mentorship-program for nye lærere.
entries.content_en:
"Can you make a living teaching yoga?" The answer is yes — but not from day one, and not in the way most people imagine. This guide gives you the real numbers, the honest timeline, and the practical plan for making yoga a sustainable career.

Yoga as a side hustle: The first 6-12 months

Most new yoga teachers start here — and that's smart. Here's what the side-hustle phase typically looks like:

  • 2-4 classes per week at one or two studios
  • Fee: 300-500 DKK per class in Copenhagen (less outside the city)
  • Monthly income: 3,000-8,000 DKK before tax
  • Time commitment: 6-12 hours/week including preparation and travel

The side-hustle phase is important because it gives you:

  1. Teaching experience without financial pressure
  2. Network — you meet studios, students, and other teachers
  3. Feedback — you discover what works and what doesn't
  4. Time to develop your teaching style

The transition period: When are you ready to scale?

The signals telling you it's time to do more:

  • Your classes are consistently full — people recommend you
  • You receive enquiries from studios you haven't contacted
  • You have private students who want more time with you
  • You feel limited by your current schedule — you want to teach more
  • Your yoga income covers at least 30-40% of your fixed expenses

The path to full-time: A realistic plan

Diversify your income streams

Full-time yoga teachers rarely live from studio classes alone. They combine:

  • Studio teaching: Steady base income
  • Private sessions: 500-1,000 DKK/session — your highest hourly rate
  • Corporate yoga: 800-2,000 DKK/session — stable and well-paid
  • Workshops: 2,000-5,000 DKK for a 3-hour workshop
  • Retreats: 5,000-15,000 DKK for a weekend
  • Online: Scalable income from video content

Numbers from reality

A full-time yoga teacher in Copenhagen with 2+ years experience:

  • Typical gross: 20,000-35,000 DKK/month
  • Experienced with corporate + retreats: 35,000-55,000 DKK/month
  • Top-tier: 55,000+ DKK/month (own studio, online platform, retreats)
"I went full-time after 18 months. It required a savings buffer of 3 months' expenses and a detailed plan. Today my income is higher than my old office job — and my quality of life is incomparable."

The hidden costs

Remember to factor in:

  • Insurance: Professional liability insurance (1,500-3,000 DKK/year)
  • Continuing education: Workshops, certifications, conferences
  • Equipment: Mat, props, clothing, music subscription
  • Transport: Between studios and corporate clients
  • Marketing: Website, social media, business cards

The honest conclusion

Can you live from yoga? Yes. Is it easy? No. Is it worth it? That depends on what you're looking for. If you want security and predictability, yoga as a side hustle is a fantastic quality-of-life booster. If you're passionate about it and willing to invest 2-3 years in building up, full-time can be the best career decision you ever make.

Our yoga teacher training includes a "Business of Yoga" module that gives you the tools most trainings lack. Or contact us to hear about our mentorship programme for new teachers.
